summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #594 where the boot-md hook was a no-op — BOOT.md never actually provided startup context to agents because the hook fired on a read-only GatewayStart event and its payload was silently discarded.
summary.outcome: Replaced the broken hook-based approach with the established workspace markdown pattern (matching SOUL.md/AGENTS.md/TOOLS.md/MEMORY.md), deleted the dead boot_md.rs plugin, and wired BOOT.md through PromptPersona into the system prompt. All tests pass, PR #614 created and two follow-up fixes pushed.

repo: Workspace markdown files (SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, TOOLS.md, MEMORY.md, now BOOT.md) follow a consistent pattern: loaded per-session via PromptPersona, injected via build_system_prompt_*() functions in crates/agents/src/prompt.rs.
GatewayStart is a read-only event that fires once at server start — returning ModifyPayload from a hook on this event is silently discarded; it cannot be used for per-session injection.

gemini-3-* models match the `id.starts_with("gemini-3")` predicate in supports_reasoning_for_model, so adding Gemini 3.x models to the provider list also generates reasoning variants, affecting model count assertions.

The CI system uses a local/xxx=success dependency chain — a single upstream failure (Local E2E Validation) cascades into many dependent job failures that appear as blockers but are not caused by the PR's changes.
The worktree at /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/yielding-asp has no remote configured, so git pull is a no-op; pushes go directly via the tracked remote branch.

code.finding: build_system_prompt_minimal_runtime call inside tests also needed the new boot_text parameter — easy to miss when updating signatures.

list_and_list_all_return_all_registered_models asserts a hardcoded model count (was 6, became 9 after Gemini 3.x added reasoning variants) — pre-existing breakage from a prior commit, not from this PR.
discover_hooks_registers_builtin_handlers test was asserting 'boot-md' still existed in both handler_names and builtin_hook_metadata — stale test caught by Greptile in PR review.
load_boot_md_for_agent follows the exact pattern of load_tools_md_for_agent: check agent-specific workspace dir first, fall back to global data_dir via or_else.

workflow: Greptile automated PR review caught the stale gateway test asserting the deleted boot-md hook was still registered — useful complement to local cargo test runs.

Running clippy only on affected crates (cargo clippy -p moltis-config -p moltis-agents -p moltis-chat -p moltis-plugins) is faster and sufficient when the cmake/C++ build environment is broken for unrelated reasons.
When CI jobs show 'current: missing' status, check if they are cascading failures from an unrelated upstream gate before investigating them as code issues.

friction: 20+ test call sites in prompt.rs needed a new None parameter inserted at position 8 — required spawning a subagent to batch-update them reliably.
Three different indentation levels for the soul_text/agents_text pattern in chat/src/lib.rs required three separate replace_all calls instead of one.
clippy --workspace failed due to a cmake/C++ build environment issue completely unrelated to the Rust changes, forcing crate-by-crate clippy runs as a workaround.
CI cascading failures from Local E2E Validation made it hard to identify the single real test failure (model count assertion) among many apparent blockers.
The pre-existing model count test breakage (introduced by the Gemini 3.x commit on main) was unrelated to this PR but still had to be fixed to get CI green.
open_items: The cmake/C++ build environment issue causing `cargo clippy --workspace` to fail should be investigated and fixed separately.
The Local E2E Validation CI gate that caused cascading failures was not investigated — its root cause is unknown and may affect future PRs.
Consider adding a lint or test that validates builtin hook metadata stays in sync with registered hooks, to catch stale assertions like the one Greptile found.
